trails.name: Mauao mount maunganui
trails.description: 3.4 km and takes about 45 minutes to walk. Suitable for strollers and wheelchairs but please take care.

trails.name: Papamoa hills
trails.description: Remember sunscreen and a hat for sunny days and although most tracks are well formed, good walking shoes are recommended, as is a reasonable level of fitness. Take a bottle of water with you as there is no drinking water supply in the Park

trails.name: Papamoa hills
trails.description: Remember sunscreen and a hat for sunny days and although most tracks are well formed, good walking shoes are recommended, as is a reasonable level of fitness. Take a bottle of water with you as there is no drinking water supply in the Park

trails.name: OTANEWAINUKU RIMU LOOP WALK
trails.description: Walk among large rimu trees in Otanewainuku Forest. You can also take a side trip to Whataroa Falls, a popular swimming hole and picnic spot.

trails.name: Kaharoa Kōkako Track
trails.description: Walk downhill through lush native forest to Onaia Stream. Keep your eyes and ears peeled for kōkako hopping through the forest canopy feeding or singing.

trails.name: Kaituna Wetland Loop Track
trails.description: This loop track meanders through the wetland. You can get up close and personal with the wetland in a viewing hide.

trails.name: Mangorewa Track
trails.description: This track passes through a fine example of lowland forest, one of the few areas left unlogged on the Mamaku Plateau.

trails.name: Otanewainuku walking tracks
trails.description: Explore Otanewainuku Forest on these short walks - there's an easier option for families with children.

trails.name: Otawa Reservoir Track
trails.description: A well defined track leads to a small clearing beside Te Puke's original water source.

trails.name: Matatā Lagoon Track
trails.description: This track is a short walk around Matatā Wildlife refuge. There’s a lot of birdlife here and great views to the islands of Moutohorā Island and Whakaari/White Island and down the East Coast.

trails.name: Kaituna Wetland Track
trails.description:
Access the Kaituna Wetland is off Kaituna Road just outside Te Puke. The walk meanders through the wetland for just over 6 km. Along the walk you can see interpretation panels which have interesting facts and information about the Lower Kaituna Wildlife Management Reserve.

The wetland has an abundant supply of waterfowl.

trails.name: Kiwi Wandering Trail
trails.description: A self-guided scavenger hunt in Whakat?ne, New Zealand, the Kiwi Capital of the World!  Find the ten life-like, life?size bronze kiwi statues which wander right through the heart of Whakat?ne.

trails.name: Latham's Hill Track
trails.description:
The walk starts with a relatively gentle climb over rolling farmland which leads up to native bush and a trig station. There you can enjoy superb views over the Rangit?iki Plains.

The main track ends at the trig station, we recommend for most walkers, especially with younger families, turning around here and returning to the car park. 

Another route descends from the trig station to the Awakeri Hot Springs complex, this section is steeper and if you're looping back to the car park, means walking along a busy section of State Highway 30.

trails.name: Te Awa a te Atua Beach
